| 2013 | HIGH | Tags/CrdtTagCollection.cs | 91-95 | sdk-audit | TOCTOU race in Set: Contains + Add + version increment + value write not atomic — concurrent Set corrupts OR-Set causal history and version vectors. Same in Remove and Merge. |
| 2014 | MEDIUM | Tags/DefaultTagPolicyEngine.cs | 75-89 | sdk-audit | `ListPoliciesAsync` does no async work — iterates sync dictionary with dummy `await Task.CompletedTask` to satisfy compiler. |
| 2015 | LOW | Tags/DefaultTagPropagationEngine.cs | 36-37 | sdk-audit | Uses plain Dictionary + lock instead of BoundedDictionary — inconsistent with SDK pattern, unbounded growth. |
| 2016 | HIGH | Tags/DefaultTagQueryApi.cs | 301-339 | sdk-audit | `CompileNotAsync` calls `GetTagDistributionAsync(int.MaxValue)` then fires full index query per tag key — unbounded O(T×K) memory accumulation, OOM on large datasets. |
| 2017 | LOW | Tags/DefaultTagQueryApi.cs | 422-428 | sdk-audit | `SortObjectKeys` switch has only `_` default arm — all sort fields silently ignored, always sorts by ObjectKey. |
| 2018 | HIGH | Tags/InMemoryTagSchemaRegistry.cs | 87-133 | sdk-audit | TOCTOU lost-update race in AddVersionAsync — two concurrent callers both read same Versions list, both add version, one silently wins losing the other's schema version. |
| 2019 | MEDIUM | Tags/InMemoryTagSchemaRegistry.cs | 87-133 | sdk-audit | (See also #664) No logging when concurrent version addition overwrites another — silent data loss on schema evolution. |
| 2020 | LOW | Tags/InMemoryTagStore.cs | 151 | sdk-audit | `FindObjectsByTagAsync` limit=0 or negative silently returns empty — no validation. |
| 2021 | HIGH | Tags/InvertedTagIndex.cs | 195-210 | sdk-audit | QueryAsync Skip has no upper-bound guard — `Skip = int.MaxValue` silently returns empty results with valid TotalCount. No negative-value validation either. |
| 2022 | MEDIUM | Tags/InvertedTagIndex.cs | 205-209 | sdk-audit | QueryAsync sorts entire candidate set O(N log N) before Skip/Take — millions of entries materialized for pagination on large deployments. |
| 2023 | HIGH | Tags/InvertedTagIndex.cs | 346 | sdk-audit | Dispose not thread-safe — non-atomic check+dispose, concurrent callers can hit ObjectDisposedException from ReaderWriterLockSlim.EnterReadLock on disposed instance. |
| 2024 | LOW | Tags/InvertedTagIndex.cs | 361 | sdk-audit | `GetShard` uses `string.GetHashCode()` — randomized per process in .NET 5+, non-deterministic shard assignment across restarts. |
| 2025 | MEDIUM | Tags/InvertedTagIndex.cs | 376-379 | sdk-audit | NotExists filter calls CollectAllObjectKeys scanning every shard — materializes all keys into HashSet before filtering. |
| 2026 | LOW | Tags/ITagAttachmentService.cs | 119 | sdk-audit | Inconsistent limit defaults — ITagAttachmentService defaults 100, ITagStore has no default. |
| 2027 | MEDIUM | Tags/TagEvents.cs | 80-88 | sdk-audit | `TagsBulkUpdatedEvent` doesn't inherit from `TagEvent` unlike all other event types — breaks polymorphic bus subscription for TagEvent consumers. |
| 2028 | MEDIUM | Tags/TagMergeStrategy.cs | 119,157 | sdk-audit | MultiValue/Union merge strategies use `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow` — violates documented determinism contract, causes CRDT convergence divergence. |
| 2029 | LOW | Tags/TagPropagationRule.cs | 109 | sdk-audit | `TransformFunc` (Func<Tag,Tag>) not serializable — silent null on deserialization across process boundaries. |
| 2030 | LOW | Tags/TagQueryExpression.cs | 176 | sdk-audit | `TagQueryRequest.Skip` has no validation — negative values silently accepted, causes ArgumentOutOfRangeException deep in query stack. |
| 2031 | MEDIUM | Tags/TagSchema.cs | 46 | sdk-audit | `TagConstraint.Pattern` not validated as valid regex — invalid pattern causes unhandled ArgumentException on every validation call instead of TagValidationError. |
| 2032 | LOW | Tags/TagSchema.cs | 81 | sdk-audit | `TagSchemaVersion.CreatedUtc` defaults to UtcNow — JSON deserialization without CreatedUtc gets current time, not original creation time. |
| 2033 | HIGH | Tags/TagSchema.cs | 120 | sdk-audit | `CurrentVersion => Versions[^1]` throws IndexOutOfRangeException on empty Versions list — no guard despite "must contain at least one" comment. |
| 2034 | HIGH | Tags/TagTypes.cs | 14 | sdk-audit | `TagKey` constructor accepts empty/null Namespace and Name — no validation on the record used as dictionary key throughout entire tag subsystem. |
| 2035 | MEDIUM | Tags/TagTypes.cs | 144-145 | sdk-audit | `GetByNamespace` uses OrdinalIgnoreCase but TagKey record equality is case-sensitive — asymmetric behavior, `GetByNamespace("System")` finds "system" but indexer doesn't. |
| 2036 | LOW | Tags/TagTypes.cs | 181 | sdk-audit | `TagCollection.GetHashCode` calls OrderBy on every computation — allocates and sorts on each hash lookup. |

| 2109 | HIGH | Utilities/BoundedDictionary.cs | 244 | sdk-audit | TryGetValue uses write lock for read operation (due to LRU MoveToFront) — all concurrent reads serialized, eliminating read parallelism. IReadOnlyDictionary consumers pay full write-lock cost. |
| 2110 | MEDIUM | Utilities/BoundedDictionary.cs | 553 | sdk-audit | `PersistAsync` sets `_pendingPersist = false` outside lock — concurrent mutation between SaveAsync completion and flag reset causes lost persist, data not written. |
| 2111 | HIGH | Utilities/BoundedDictionary.cs | 608-614 | sdk-audit | Silent catch in OnDebounceElapsed PersistAsync — fault swallowed with only a comment, no logging. Silent data-loss when persistence repeatedly fails. Same in BoundedList:240 and BoundedQueue:233. |
| 2112 | MEDIUM | Utilities/BoundedDictionary.cs | 702 | sdk-audit | Dispose uses `Task.Run(...).GetAwaiter().GetResult()` — sync-over-async blocks calling thread for full persist duration, deadlock risk in threadpool-starved environments. |
| 2113 | LOW | Utilities/PluginDetails.cs | 16 | sdk-audit | `PluginDescriptor.Tags` is mutable `List<string>` on init-only class — external mutation corrupts plugin metadata. Should be IReadOnlyList. |
| 2114 | MEDIUM | Utilities/PluginDetails.cs | 286 | sdk-audit | `IsAllowedPayloadType` allows any `IEnumerable<string>` implementation through — custom classes with arbitrary state bypass type allowlist. |
| 2115 | LOW | Validation/Guards.cs | 107 | sdk-audit | `MaxLength` returns `value!` when input is null — suppresses nullable warning but returns null through non-nullable return type. |
| 2116 | MEDIUM | Validation/InputValidation.cs | 261 | sdk-audit | `ValidateSecurityRules` uses uncached `typeof(T).GetProperties()` reflection on every call — GC pressure on hot validation path. |
| 2117 | LOW | Validation/SqlSecurity.cs | 55-59 | sdk-audit | `_cacheMaxSize` parameter accepted but ignored — cache grows unbounded regardless of configured limit. |
| 2118 | MEDIUM | Validation/SqlSecurity.cs | 75 | sdk-audit | Cache key uses `object.GetHashCode()` on SqlSecurityOptions — reference-based hash means every new options instance misses cache, unbounded cache growth. |
| 2119 | LOW | Validation/SqlSecurity.cs | 378 | sdk-audit | `IsReservedWord` allocates new `HashSet<string>` per call — should be static readonly. |
| 2120 | LOW | Validation/SqlSecurity.cs | 575 | sdk-audit | `ValidateOperator` allocates new `HashSet<string>` per call — same pattern. |
| 2121 | MEDIUM | Validation/ValidationMiddleware.cs | 453-464 | sdk-audit | CommandInjectionFilter blocks bare `<` and `>` characters — breaks legitimate SQL comparison operators and XML content in queries. |
| 2122 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/AdaptiveIndexEngine.cs | 303-307 | sdk-audit | Old index implementing IAsyncDisposable not disposed when morph swaps to new index — resource leak on every index morph transition. |
| 2123 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/AlexLearnedIndex.cs | 457-495 | sdk-audit | `_consecutiveLowHitWindows` is a plain int incremented/reset from multiple threads without Interlocked — race causes missed deactivation threshold. |
| 2124 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ArtIndex.cs | 77-84 | sdk-audit | Count increment ordering brittle on duplicate-key exception — if `_items.TryAdd` succeeds but `_count` increment fails (ThreadAbortException in older runtime), count diverges from actual item count permanently. |
| 2125 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ArtIndex.cs | 149 | sdk-audit | Gratuitous `await Task.CompletedTask` in synchronous path — unnecessary allocation. |
| 2126 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 138-148 | sdk-audit | WAL transaction opened on write but never rolled back on error — partial writes leave WAL in inconsistent state, next recovery replays corrupted entries. |
| 2127 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 214-251 | sdk-audit | `UpdateAsync` always returns true regardless of whether the key existed — contract lie, callers cannot distinguish update from insert. |
| 2128 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 223 | sdk-audit | Upsert message type used in UpdateAsync — contract lie, named "Update" but semantically an upsert. |
| 2129 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 637,711 | sdk-audit | O(n×m) LINQ scan per leaf vs pending messages — `_pendingMessages.Where(m => IsInRange(m.Key, leaf))` scans all pending for every leaf during flush. |
| 2130 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 637 | sdk-audit | Flush writes pending messages into leaf nodes but doesn't clear them from pending list within same lock — concurrent reader sees both pending and flushed copy. |
| 2131 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTree.cs | 763-805 | sdk-audit | Mutable cached node objects shared across concurrent read/write paths — write mutations visible to in-flight reads, causes corrupted traversal results. |
| 2132 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeForest.cs | 169-177 | sdk-audit | Shard index computed outside lock, used inside lock — concurrent AddAsync/RemoveAsync on same key can route to wrong shard between the two operations. |
| 2133 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeForest.cs | 243 | sdk-audit | `_shards.Count` accessed without lock in property getter — stale read possible during concurrent shard creation. |
| 2134 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeForest.cs | 276-289 | sdk-audit | Full materialization before first yield — `GetAllAsync` loads all keys from all shards into memory before returning. |
| 2135 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeMessage.cs | 143-145 | sdk-audit | No max bounds on `keyLen` from deserialization — attacker-crafted value causes `new byte[keyLen]` OOM allocation. |
| 2136 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeNode.cs | 125-135 | sdk-audit | Silent truncation when entries exceed blockSize — data beyond block boundary silently dropped during serialization, permanent data loss on flush. |
| 2137 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BeTreeNode.cs | 213-215 | sdk-audit | Same unchecked `keyLen` on deserialization — identical OOM vector as BeTreeMessage. |
| 2138 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BloofiFilter.cs | 36 | sdk-audit | Plain `object` lock serializes all reads and writes — single global lock eliminates all concurrency in a read-heavy probabilistic data structure. |
| 2139 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BloofiFilter.cs | 507 | sdk-audit | `IsLeaf` invariant undocumented — tree correctness depends on fragile child-count check with no assertion or comment. |
| 2140 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BwTree.cs | 224-253 | sdk-audit | Epoch not guaranteed released if enumerator abandoned mid-iteration — leaked epoch blocks garbage collection of all subsequent delta chains. |
| 2141 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BwTree.cs | 465-490 | sdk-audit | `GetHashCode()` used as key equality proxy — hash collisions silently drop entries. Two distinct keys with same hash treated as duplicates. |
| 2142 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BwTree.cs | 465 | sdk-audit | Hash-based page lookup O(1) amortized but worst-case unbounded on collision chains — no rehash threshold. |
| 2143 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/BwTreeMappingTable.cs | 115-118 | sdk-audit | `pageId` long → int truncation — `Interlocked.Increment(ref _nextPageId)` returns long but used as int index, overflow after 2B pages. |
| 2144 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ClockSiTransaction.cs | 416-433 | sdk-audit | `PrepareShardAsync` always returns true (stub) — no actual conflict detection implemented, distributed transactions silently commit conflicting writes. |
| 2145 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ClockSiTransaction.cs | 446-454 | sdk-audit | Unsynchronized Dispose — no disposed guard, concurrent Dispose+Commit can corrupt transaction state. |
| 2146 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/CrushPlacement.cs | 98-103 | sdk-audit | `CrushBucket.IsDown` bool read/written across threads without synchronization — stale reads cause routing to downed nodes. |
| 2147 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DirectPointerIndex.cs | 146-152 | sdk-audit | `RecommendLevelAsync` dead-code conditional — always returns same level regardless of input, recommendation logic is no-op. |
| 2148 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DisruptorMessageBus.cs | 205 | sdk-audit | Sync-over-async `.GetAwaiter().GetResult()` in PublishAsync hot path — blocks threadpool thread, deadlock risk under load. |
| 2149 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DisruptorMessageBus.cs | 317-349 | sdk-audit | Channel mode doesn't fan out to multiple subscribers — only one subscriber receives each message, others silently miss it. |
| 2150 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DisruptorMessageBus.cs | 383-390 | sdk-audit | `RemoveSubscription` doesn't await ReaderTask — fire-and-forget cleanup, reader may still be processing when caller assumes unsubscribed. |
| 2151 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DisruptorRingBuffer.cs | 374-394 | sdk-audit | TOCTOU in `MinimumGatingSequence` — reads multiple sequence values without snapshot, result inconsistent if producers advance between reads. |
| 2152 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DisruptorRingBuffer.cs | 598-668 | sdk-audit | `Sequence` and `ProcessedCount` are plain long fields read/written across threads without volatile or Interlocked — torn reads on 32-bit, stale values on 64-bit causing consumer to skip or re-read entries. |
| 2153 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/DistributedRoutingIndex.cs | 433-445 | sdk-audit | Naive k-way merge — sequential merge of k sorted streams, should use heap-based merge for O(n log k). |
| 2154 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/EpochManager.cs | 160-193 | sdk-audit | `TryCollect` O(n) scan over all threads every collection cycle — performance cliff with many concurrent threads. |
| 2155 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ExtendibleHashBucket.cs | 132-148 | sdk-audit | Deserialized `count` not validated against buffer bounds — crafted input causes out-of-bounds read. |
| 2156 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ExtendibleHashTable.cs | 194-232 | sdk-audit | Unbounded recursion in `InsertInternal` — split-then-retry loop with no depth limit, pathological key distribution causes StackOverflowException. |
| 2157 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/GpuVectorKernels.cs | 112-116 | sdk-audit | Silent catch in IlgpuVectorAccelerator constructor — GPU init failure swallowed, no logging, falls through to CPU without any indication. |
| 2158 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/GpuVectorKernels.cs | 129-149,298-308,322-333 | sdk-audit | Both IlgpuVectorAccelerator and CuvsVectorAccelerator are GPU stubs executing CPU code while reporting `IsAvailable=true` — contract lie, callers believe GPU acceleration is active but get CPU fallback performance. |
| 2159 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/GpuVectorKernels.cs | 492-514 | sdk-audit | Silent catch in VectorAcceleratorFactory.Create — same pattern, all GPU errors silently swallowed. |
| 2160 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/GpuVectorKernels.cs:combined | - | sdk-audit | Multiple silent catch blocks across GPU initialization paths — all swallow exceptions without logging or metrics. |
| 2161 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/HilbertCurveEngine.cs | 525-554 | sdk-audit | 1M point materialization — `GetAllPointsInRegion` generates all Hilbert indices for region before filtering, extreme memory for large volumes. |
| 2162 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/HilbertPartitioner.cs | 65 | sdk-audit | Hardcoded 2-dimensional in general-purpose API — `DimensionCount` parameter accepted but `HilbertToXY` only works for 2D, silently returns garbage for higher dimensions. |
| 2163 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/HnswIndex.cs | 582-619 | sdk-audit | O(n²) `SearchLayer` — `results.Max` called inside inner loop, each Max is O(k) over results list. Should use priority queue. |
| 2164 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/HnswIndex.cs | 724 | sdk-audit | O(n) `AddNeighbor` duplicate check via `List.Contains` — called for every edge insertion, degrades to O(n²) on dense graphs. |
| 2165 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IndexMirroring.cs | 288-293 | sdk-audit | Silent catch in `PropagateWriteAsync` — sets health to Degraded and enqueues retry but never logs the exception. Operator has zero visibility into failure type. |
| 2166 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IndexMorphAdvisor.cs | 76,225 | sdk-audit | `DisabledLevels ??=` inside lock but property publicly mutable — external assignment can race. |
| 2167 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IndexMorphAdvisor.cs | 253-269 | sdk-audit | `EvaluateAsync` named Async but does no async work and no interface forces the signature — misleading. |
| 2168 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IndexTiering.cs | 79-83 | sdk-audit | Timer callback `_ = ManageTiersAsync()` — discarded Task, no mechanism to verify callback completes before DisposeAsync returns. |
| 2169 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IndexTiering.cs | 80,125,137 | sdk-audit | Fire-and-forget `_ = PromoteAsync(...)` uses caller's request-scoped CancellationToken — cancelled requests silently abandon promotion, key stuck in wrong tier. |
| 2170 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IoUringBlockDevice.cs | 58,633,638 | sdk-audit | `_disposed` is plain bool with no synchronization — concurrent Dispose+ReadBlock race causes use-after-free on native memory pointers. Needs `Interlocked.Exchange` guard. |
| 2171 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/IoUringBlockDevice.cs | 133-138 | sdk-audit | `RegisterBuffers` failure silently ignored but read/write unconditionally use `PrepReadFixed`/`PrepWriteFixed` — every I/O fails with EFAULT after registration failure. |
| 2172 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/LearnedShardRouter.cs | 209-212 | sdk-audit | TOCTOU in `DecrementShardCount` — non-atomic read-check-decrement can drive count to -1, corrupting shard load tracking. |
| 2173 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/MorphTransition.cs | 71,98-107 | sdk-audit | `_state` field not volatile — monitoring threads may observe stale MorphTransitionState values. |
| 2174 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/MorphTransitionEngine.cs | 43,433-434 | sdk-audit | `_currentTransition` read without volatile/lock from `GetProgress()` and `EnqueueDualWrite()` — stale null on ARM/NUMA silently discards dual writes. |
| 2175 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/MorphTransitionEngine.cs | 408-416 | sdk-audit | OperationCanceledException caught but no WAL abort marker written — crash recovery may resume aborted transition. |
| 2176 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/MorphTransitionEngine.cs | 493-498 | sdk-audit | `JournalEntry.TransactionId` hardcoded to -1 — prevents crash-recovery correlation with transaction scope. |
| 2177 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/MorphTransitionEngine.cs | 531-539 | sdk-audit | Rule 13 stub — `CreateIndexForLevel` throws NotSupportedException for levels 3-6 (BeTree, LearnedIndex, BeTreeForest, DistributedRouting). Blocks production morph at scale. |
| 2178 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/PersistentExtentTree.cs | 62,72,706-709 | sdk-audit | `_disposed` plain bool, DisposeAsync has no idempotency guard — concurrent callers double-dispose ReaderWriterLockSlim. |
| 2179 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/PersistentExtentTree.cs | 461 | sdk-audit | `AllocateBlock` return value not validated — error sentinel (-1/0) used as block number, corrupts superblock. |
| 2180 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/PersistentExtentTree.cs | 685-702 | sdk-audit | `SnapshotExtents` destructively extracts and reinserts all extents O(n log n) — mutates live tree during what should be a read. |
| 2181 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/ProductQuantizer.cs | 229-255 | sdk-audit | `Deserialize` reads `dimension`, `m`, `k`, `subDim` from untrusted stream without bounds validation — crafted input causes OOM via arbitrary allocation. |
| 2182 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/SimdOperations.cs | 372 | sdk-audit | `XxHash64Simd` name implies SIMD but implementation is pure scalar — contract lie, misleads callers. |
| 2183 | LOW | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/TrainedZstdDictionary.cs | 582-584 | sdk-audit | `dictSize` read as uint from untrusted block device, cast to int without bounds check — overflow to negative causes invalid block reads. |
| 2184 | HIGH | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/TrainedZstdDictionary.cs | 849-850 | sdk-audit | `DictionaryRetrainer.RetrainCount++` and `LastRetrainTime` written from timer thread without volatile/Interlocked — torn reads from monitoring. `_baselineRatio` double also unsynchronized. |
| 2185 | MEDIUM | VDE/AdaptiveIndex/TrainedZstdDictionary.cs | 868-871 | sdk-audit | `DictionaryRetrainer.Dispose()` doesn't dispose `_currentCompressor` (IDisposable) — final compressor leaked. |
| 2186 | MEDIUM | VDE/Allocation/AllocationGroup.cs | 78-98 | sdk-audit | `FragmentationRatio` iterates every block individually O(n) with read lock held — 32K iterations for 128MB group, blocks all concurrent writes. |
| 2187 | MEDIUM | VDE/Allocation/ExtentTree.cs | 532-534 | sdk-audit | `TryMergeOrRedistributeAsync` frees wrong block — sibling's original block number not captured before mutation, permanent block leak on every merge. |

| 2189 | HIGH | VDE/BlockAllocation/ExtentTree.cs | 64-65 | sdk-audit | Merged extent block count double-counts `count` — formula `(toMerge.Max(EndBlock) - mergedStart) + count` inflates size when adjacent on both sides, corrupts free-space accounting and causes double-allocation. |
| 2190 | HIGH | VDE/BlockAllocation/FreeSpaceManager.cs | 92-109 | sdk-audit | `AllocateExtent` extent-found path has vestigial loop that frees-then-reallocates arbitrary blocks, then discards result and calls `AllocateExtent` again — bitmap/extent-tree desynchronization, data corruption. |
| 2191 | MEDIUM | VDE/BlockAllocation/FreeSpaceManager.cs | 131,151 | sdk-audit | `FreeBlock`/`FreeExtent` use `_lock.Wait()` alongside async callers using `WaitAsync` — deadlock risk if called from async continuation. |
| 2192 | HIGH | VDE/BlockExport/ZeroCopyBlockReader.cs | 36,200-206 | sdk-audit | `_disposed` plain bool with no volatile/Interlocked — concurrent Dispose+Read race. Same in ArcCacheL2Mmap:36 and ArcCacheL3NVMe:38. |
| 2193 | HIGH | VDE/BlockExport/ZeroCopyBlockReader.cs | 63-75 | sdk-audit | MemoryMappedViewAccessor held open for object lifetime after full file read into managed buffer — wastes OS handle, prevents file release on Windows. Accessor unused after constructor. |
| 2194 | MEDIUM | VDE/BlockExport/ZeroCopyBlockReader.cs | 74-75 | sdk-audit | Entire VDE file read into managed byte[] in constructor — LOH allocation, Gen2 GC pressure, defeats zero-copy purpose. |
| 2195 | HIGH | VDE/Cache/ArcCacheL3NVMe.cs | 319-344 | sdk-audit | Double-checked locking on non-volatile `_initialized` — stale reads on ARM/weakly-ordered CPUs. Same in ArcCacheL2Mmap:137. |
| 2196 | HIGH | VDE/Cache/ArcCacheL3NVMe.cs | 339-343 | sdk-audit | Silent catch in `EnsureInitialized` — FileStream construction failure swallowed with zero logging, L3 cache silently disabled for entire lifetime. |
| 2197 | MEDIUM | VDE/Compatibility/VdeMigrationEngine.cs | 279-293 | sdk-audit | Partial-batch short-read writes truncated data but `blocksCopied` increments by full batch — silent data corruption on migration. |
| 2198 | MEDIUM | VDE/Compression/PerExtentCompressor.cs | 144-149 | sdk-audit | BrotliStream decompression truncation silently returns zero-padded buffer — caller cannot distinguish success from corruption. |
| 2199 | LOW | VDE/Concurrency/StripedWriteLock.cs | 54-59 | sdk-audit | `AcquireAsync(null)` throws NullReferenceException instead of ArgumentNullException; stripe collision semantics undocumented. |
| 2200 | HIGH | VDE/Container/ContainerFile.cs | 17 | sdk-audit | `_disposed` plain bool without volatile in both — concurrent async operations may not see disposal on weakly-ordered hardware. |

| 2258 | HIGH | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 67-96,244-282 | sdk-audit | `ReaderWriterLockSlim` acquired synchronously then `await` called inside the lock body — the lock is thread-affine and will be released on a different thread, causing `SynchronizationLockException` or silent corruption. |
| 2259 | HIGH | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 146-151 | sdk-audit | Explicit rollback stub: comment says "in a full implementation, would revert changes" — delete operations silently corrupt the tree on failure. |
| 2260 | HIGH | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 147-156 | sdk-audit | `_rootBlockNumber` mutated before `FlushAsync` — if flush fails, the in-memory root pointer is permanently wrong and the B-Tree is broken for the rest of the session with no rollback. |
| 2261 | MEDIUM | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 336-340 | sdk-audit | Cache eviction uses `OrderBy(x => x.Value.lastAccess)` on 1000 entries under a write lock — O(n log n) sort blocks all concurrent readers during eviction. |
| 2262 | MEDIUM | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 354-382 | sdk-audit | WAL `JournalEntry.BeforeImage` is always set to `null` — undo-based recovery is impossible, WAL can only redo, not rollback. |
| 2263 | HIGH | VDE/Index/BTree.cs | 532-559 | sdk-audit | `DeleteFromNodeAsync` is missing B-Tree rebalancing logic — `IsUnderflow` property is defined on `BTreeNode` but never called. Deletes eventually violate the B-Tree invariant (minimum fill), degrading to a linked list. |
| 2264 | MEDIUM | VDE/Index/BTreeNode.cs | 260-316 | sdk-audit | `KeyCount` deserialized from untrusted on-disk data is not validated against `MaxKeys` — corrupt value causes out-of-bounds array access. |

| 2277 | MEDIUM | VDE/Journal/CheckpointManager.cs | 118-127 | sdk-audit | `Dispose()` method exists but class doesn't implement `IDisposable` — `using` statements won't compile, `SemaphoreSlim` leaked if caller doesn't call `Dispose()` manually. |
| 2278 | MEDIUM | VDE/Journal/WalTransaction.cs | 279-286 | sdk-audit | `DisposeAsync` auto-abort catch swallows all errors with no logging — failed abort leaves orphaned `BeginTransaction` marker in WAL, confusing `ReplayAsync`. |
| 2279 | CRITICAL | VDE/Journal/WriteAheadLog.cs | 46-64 | sdk-audit | `_headBlock` and `_tailBlock` read with separate `Interlocked.Read` calls — torn pair between reads allows `WalUtilization` and `GetAvailableBlocks` to return inconsistent values. `EnsureSpaceAsync` uses torn utilization to decide checkpointing, risking writes into a full WAL. |
| 2280 | CRITICAL | VDE/Journal/WriteAheadLog.cs | 311 | sdk-audit | `blockOffset = (_headBlock == 1 ? 0 : 0)` — trivially constant expression means every entry starts at byte 0 of its block. | 2301 | HIGH | VDE/ModuleManagement/WalJournaledRegionWriter.cs | 436-439 | sdk-audit | `UpdateWalHeaderAsync` reads WAL header with synchronous `_vdeStream.Read()` — called after every WAL commit, blocking async continuation on every superblock/bitmap update. |
| 2302 | HIGH | VDE/Mvcc/MvccGarbageCollector.cs | 174-178 | sdk-audit | Silent catch swallows all exceptions including `IOException` and `OperationCanceledException` — persistent I/O error causes infinite spin with zero reclamation and no operator signal. |
| 2303 | LOW | VDE/Mvcc/MvccIsolationEnforcer.cs | 201-252 | sdk-audit | O(n) scan of `_committedWrites` on every commit — quadratic degradation under sustained throughput without aggressive pruning. |
| 2304 | MEDIUM | VDE/Mvcc/MvccManager.cs | 130 | sdk-audit | Serializable conflict check compares `committedTxId` (transaction counter) against `tx.SnapshotSequence` (WAL sequence number) — two independent counters, comparison is meaningless. Produces spurious aborts or misses real conflicts. |
| 2305 | MEDIUM | VDE/Mvcc/MvccManager.cs | 247-260 | sdk-audit | Snapshot isolation read path returns `currentBuffer` unconditionally without walking version chain — `MvccVersionStore.GetVersionChainAsync` exists but is never invoked. Snapshot isolation does not actually isolate. |
| 2306 | HIGH | VDE/Mvcc/MvccVersionStore.cs | 46-51 | sdk-audit | `_nextFreeBlock` (plain `long`) read without volatile/Interlocked in public `UsedBlocks`/`FreeBlocks` properties — on 32-bit hosts non-atomic; on 64-bit hosts may read stale cached value. |
| 2307 | LOW | VDE/Mvcc/MvccVersionStore.cs | 219 | sdk-audit | `GetVersionChainAsync` allocates `new byte[blockSize]` per chain link in loop — unbounded GC pressure for long version chains; should use `ArrayPool`. |
| 2308 | LOW | VDE/PhysicalDevice/DeviceDiscoveryService.cs | 314,538 | sdk-audit | `SupportsVolatileWriteCache` hardcoded `false` on both Linux and Windows — callers conservatively flush always, suboptimal for devices that support write-back caching. |
| 2309 | MEDIUM | VDE/PhysicalDevice/DeviceDiscoveryService.cs | 364-372 | sdk-audit | `ReadSysfsFileAsync` catch-all swallows `OperationCanceledException` — cancellation token becomes non-functional during Linux device discovery. |
| 2310 | LOW | VDE/PhysicalDevice/DeviceDiscoveryService.cs | 565-570 | sdk-audit | SATA fixed disk unconditionally mapped to `MediaType.SSD` — spinning rust on SATA misclassified, causes wrong tier placement (warm instead of cold). |
| 2311 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/AnonymizationTableRegion.cs | 323-343 | sdk-audit | Serialize/Deserialize block-boundary logic: `blockPayloadEnd` doesn't account for block-0 header in overflow check — last entry near boundary skipped to next block unnecessarily, wasting space. |
| 2312 | LOW | VDE/Regions/AnonymizationTableRegion.cs | 390 | sdk-audit | `mappingCount` from buffer not upper-bounded — corrupt value produces `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` instead of `InvalidDataException`. |
| 2313 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/AuditLogRegion.cs | 95 | sdk-audit | `ReadFrom` reads `detailsLen` from buffer and uses it to slice without bounds check — corrupt/adversarial value causes uncontrolled `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`. `MaxDetailsLength` (256) not enforced during deserialization. |
| 2314 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/AuditLogRegion.cs | 137,188 | sdk-audit | `_entries` (`List<AuditLogEntry>`) and `NextSequenceNumber` have no synchronization — concurrent `Append` calls corrupt the list, produce duplicate sequence numbers, and break the hash chain that provides tamper-evidence. |
| 2315 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/ComplianceVaultRegion.cs | 139-146 | sdk-audit | `issuerLen` and `notesLen` read from buffer and used for span slicing without validation — negative or oversized values produce raw exceptions instead of `InvalidDataException`. DoS via large allocation from malicious `issuerLen`. |
| 2316 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/ComputeCodeCacheRegion.cs | 117-120 | sdk-audit | `entryPointLen` (ushort, up to 65535) not bounded against `MaxEntryPointNameLength` (128) during deserialization — 64KB allocation per corrupt entry. `blockCount` and `moduleSizeBytes` also unvalidated. |
| 2317 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/IntegrityTreeRegion.cs | 448-453 | sdk-audit | `leafCount` has no upper bound — values near `Int32.MaxValue` cause integer overflow in `NextPowerOfTwo` computation and `new byte[totalNodes][]` allocation, producing `IndexOutOfRangeException` or OOM. |
| 2318 | LOW | VDE/Regions/MetricsLogRegion.cs | 136 | sdk-audit | `_samples.Count` (int) compared to `(long)(MaxCapacitySamples * CompactionThreshold)` — if `MaxCapacitySamples > Int32.MaxValue`, compaction never triggers since `List.Count` can't exceed `int.MaxValue`. |

| 2323 | HIGH | VDE/Regions/SnapshotTableRegion.cs | 127-129 | sdk-audit | `labelLen` (ushort) from disk not validated against remaining buffer or `MaxLabelLength` — corrupt value causes `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` on deserialization. |
| 2324 | LOW | VDE/Regions/TagIndexRegion.cs | 466-469 | sdk-audit | `CountNodes` is O(N) recursive traversal on every `RequiredBlocks` call — should maintain running counter. |

| 2330 | LOW | VDE/Sql/ArrowColumnarBridge.cs | 282-289 | sdk-audit | `AsReadOnlyMemory<T>` documented as "zero-copy" but allocates and copies — misleading comment and unnecessary GC pressure. |

| 2333 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/ColumnarRegionEngine.cs | 245-253 | sdk-audit | Non-atomic disk + memory update for `RowGroupCount`: in-memory `ConcurrentDictionary` replaced then disk block rewritten without lock — concurrent appends lose row group counts. |
| 2334 | MEDIUM | VDE/Sql/ColumnarRegionEngine.cs | 283-295 | sdk-audit | `dataLen` from disk not bounded before multi-block allocation — corrupt value causes OOM with no diagnostic. |
| 2335 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/ColumnarRegionEngine.cs | 450-455 | sdk-audit | `HashCode.Combine` (non-stable, randomized per-process) used for persistent zone map min/max on String/Binary columns — values meaningless after process restart, causing incorrect query results. |
| 2336 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/ParquetVdeIntegration.cs | 183 | sdk-audit | Extent start block hardcoded as `100 + rowGroupIndex * batch.ColumnCount` — magic number unrelated to actual block allocation. Zone maps point to wrong physical addresses. |
| 2337 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/PredicatePushdownPlanner.cs | 380-434 | sdk-audit | Three helpers hardcode `valueWidth = 4` for all column types — Int64/Float64/Bool/DateTime columns read wrong bytes. Comment: "assume int32 = 4 bytes as default". |
| 2338 | MEDIUM | VDE/Sql/PredicatePushdownPlanner.cs | 401 | sdk-audit | `IsNull => rowValue == 0` — uses zero as null sentinel. Legitimate zero values incorrectly treated as null. Comment: "Simplified null check". |
| 2339 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/PreparedQueryCache.cs | 150-163 | sdk-audit | `PreparedQuery.RecordExecution` reads `ExecutionCount`, computes rolling average, writes back — three non-atomic fields outside any lock. Concurrent calls corrupt stats. |
| 2340 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/PreparedQueryCache.cs | 196-206 | sdk-audit | Three static `Regex` patterns compiled without `matchTimeout` — malicious SQL triggers catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) blocking thread indefinitely. |
| 2341 | HIGH | VDE/Sql/PreparedQueryCache.cs | 270-275 | sdk-audit | `Interlocked.Increment` inside `lock` — confused threading model. Stats fields are plain `long` (not volatile); any future lock-free read on 32-bit platform is a silent data race. |
| 2342 | LOW | VDE/Sql/SpillToDiskOperator.cs | 353,514,533,587 | sdk-audit | Base64 encode/decode for every dictionary key lookup — unnecessary CPU/GC overhead per row in large aggregation workloads. |
| 2343 | MEDIUM | VDE/Sql/SpillToDiskOperator.cs | 410-414 | sdk-audit | Deserialization `break`s on malformed spill block entry with no logging — silently drops remaining entries. Aggregate queries return incomplete results with no error signal. |
| 2344 | MEDIUM | VDE/Sql/ZoneMapIndex.cs | 180,207-228 | sdk-audit | Unsynchronized `Dictionary<long, ZoneMapEntry>` accessed from concurrent async paths — parallel ingestion corrupts dictionary or throws `InvalidOperationException`. |
| 2345 | MEDIUM | VDE/Sql/ZoneMapIndex.cs | 332-363 | sdk-audit | `entryCount` from disk header not upper-bounded — corrupt value causes runaway I/O reading thousands of non-existent blocks. |
| 2346 | HIGH | VDE/VdeHealthReport.cs | 88-89 | sdk-audit | Capacity computed with hardcoded `4096` block size — comment: "Assuming 4K blocks; should use actual BlockSize". All capacity metrics wrong for non-4K configurations. |
| 2347 | MEDIUM | VDE/VdeStorageStrategy.cs | 63 | sdk-audit | `ContainerPath` defaults to relative path `"datawarehouse.dwvd"` — resolves to unpredictable CWD. No absolute-path validation. Data silently created in wrong location. |

| 2361 | MEDIUM | VDE/VirtualDiskEngine.cs | 654 | sdk-audit | `scanResult.Events` not null-checked before `.Select()` — `NullReferenceException` in integrity scan path. |
| 2362 | HIGH | VDE/VirtualDiskEngine.cs | 693-722 | sdk-audit | `DisposeAsync` sets `_disposed = true` then disposes subsystems non-atomically — concurrent in-flight `StoreAsync` accesses partially-disposed `ContainerFile`, corrupting writes. |

| 1 | MEDIUM | Debug.WriteLine in Release | 6+ files | agent-scan | Catch blocks use Debug.WriteLine compiled out in Release — silent failures |
| 2 | HIGH | Fire-and-forget (_= Task.Run()) | 6+ AEDS locations + multiple plugins | agent-scan | Unobserved exceptions; process crash risk |
| 3 | HIGH | HttpClient per-connection | ~120+ connector files | agent-scan | All HTTP-based strategies create new HttpClient() per connection — socket exhaustion |
| 4 | LOW | Missing IsProductionReady flag | Multiple stub strategies | agent-scan | Stubs not identified as non-production at registration time |
| 5 | HIGH | Missing MarkDisconnected() | ~40+ connector files | agent-scan | DisconnectCoreAsync disposes connection but doesn't mark handle as disconnected |
| 6 | MEDIUM | ValidateCoreAsync always returns Valid | Multiple data format strategies | agent-scan | Validation is bypassed |

| 34 | LOW | TamperProof (27 files) | — | agent-scan | 27 files not fully audited — follow-up audit recommended |
| 35 | HIGH | TamperProof/Pipeline/ReadPhaseHandlers.cs | 113,162 | sdk-audit | `new StreamReader(stream)` not disposed in `LoadManifestAsync` and `FindLatestVersionAsync` — internal buffers leaked. |
| 36 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Pipeline/ReadPhaseHandlers.cs | 114-119 | sdk-audit | Deserialized `TamperProofManifest` not validated — null `RaidConfiguration`/`Shards`/`FinalContentHash` causes NullReferenceException deep in reconstruction. |
| 37 | HIGH | TamperProof/Pipeline/ReadPhaseHandlers.cs | 170-173 | sdk-audit | Version index fallback `catch { }` too broad — `JsonException`, `OutOfMemoryException` silently fall through to O(1000) probe loop. |
| 38 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Pipeline/ReadPhaseHandlers.cs | 179-200 | sdk-audit | `FindLatestVersionAsync` fallback probes up to 1000 versions sequentially — O(n) storage round trips on cold read path. |
| 39 | HIGH | TamperProof/Pipeline/ReadPhaseHandlers.cs | 583-619 | sdk-audit | `ReconstructMissingShards` uses XOR — comment: "Simple XOR reconstruction for demonstration — Production would use Reed-Solomon". Only recovers single shard failure; RAID-6+ configs silently produce incorrect data. |
| 40 | HIGH | TamperProof/Pipeline/WritePhaseHandlers.cs | 470 | sdk-audit | XOR parity stub — comment: "simple XOR for now, would use Reed-Solomon in production". Same pattern in `RecoveryService.cs:471`. Multi-parity protection severely less than advertised. |
| 41 | HIGH | TamperProof/Scaling/TamperProofScalingManager.cs | 107,488 | sdk-audit | `_scanPaused` (bool) and `_currentBpState` (enum) not volatile — tight polling loop may cache stale value, spinning forever. |
| 42 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Services/AuditTrailService.cs | 122 | sdk-audit | `LogOperationAsync` no null-check on `operation.Details` — null details produce incorrect hash, breaking audit chain integrity. |
| 43 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/BackgroundIntegrityScanner.cs | 134 | sdk-audit | `Task.Run(...)` launch-time faults not observed at call site — `_scanTask` stored but not awaited until `StopAsync`. |
| 44 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Services/BackgroundIntegrityScanner.cs | 362-376 | sdk-audit | `corruptedBlockDetails.Add(result)` outside lock — single-threaded now but racy if parallelized. |
| 45 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/BackgroundIntegrityScanner.cs | 475,537,553,566 | sdk-audit | `new StreamReader(stream)` not disposed at 4 locations — non-deterministic handle retention under high scan throughput. |
| 46 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/BackgroundIntegrityScanner.cs | 484-488 | sdk-audit | `DiscoverBlocksAsync` bare `catch` — any exception silently falls through to empty block dictionary. Scan cycle processes zero blocks with no diagnostic. |
| 47 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Services/BackgroundIntegrityScanner.cs | 498-502 | sdk-audit | `blockIds.Contains(pendingId)` on `List<Guid>` — O(n) per lookup, O(n²) total. Should use `HashSet<Guid>`. |
| 48 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/ComplianceReportingService.cs | 764-768 | sdk-audit | `ComputeBlockHash` returns SHA-256 of GUID bytes only — comment: "Placeholder - in production, would compute actual content hash". Attestation Merkle roots built from GUID hashes, not real data. |
| 49 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/ComplianceReportingService.cs | 779 | sdk-audit | Attestation HMAC key derived from `"attestation-key-{WormMode}"` — fully deterministic from enum value. Attacker can forge attestations. |
| 50 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/ComplianceReportingService.cs | 816-819 | sdk-audit | `ParseAttestationToken` bare `catch { return (null, string.Empty); }` — parse failures invisible to forensics. |
| 51 | CRITICAL | TamperProof/Services/OrphanCleanupService.cs | 497-499 | sdk-audit | `Dispose(bool)` calls `DisposeAsync().AsTask().Wait()` — sync-over-async deadlock under SynchronizationContext (ASP.NET, test runners). |
| 52 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/RecoveryService.cs | 651-654 | sdk-audit | WORM recovery passes `expectedIntegrityHash` as `actualHash` — incident record shows false "expected == actual", hiding corruption from auditors. |
| 53 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/SealService.cs | 181,710 | sdk-audit | `_rangeSeals` (`List`) guarded by `_rangeSealLock`, but early return inside lock in `SealRangeAsync` bypasses `PersistRangeSealAsync` — partial visibility. |
| 54 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/SealService.cs | 228-256 | sdk-audit | TOCTOU on `_blockSeals.ContainsKey()` then `TryAdd()` — concurrent seal attempts can produce non-deterministic winner. |
| 55 | LOW | TamperProof/Services/SealService.cs | 401 | sdk-audit | `SealRangeAsync` returns `sealedCount: 1` always — comment: "For now, we return 1 to indicate the range seal itself". |
| 56 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/SealService.cs | 418-421 | sdk-audit | `IsSealedAsync` ignores range seals — comment: "For now, we only check direct seals". Range-sealed blocks not protected from modification. |
| 57 | HIGH | TamperProof/Services/SealService.cs | 733-736 | sdk-audit | `GetCurrentPrincipal()` `catch { // Ignore }` — security exception swallowed, all audit entries attributed to "system". |
| 58 | HIGH | TamperProof/Storage/AzureWormStorage.cs | 195-275 | sdk-audit | Entire class is simulation — uses in-memory `Dictionary<string, AzureBlobRecord>` instead of Azure SDK. Comment: "Simulated storage for development/testing (in production, would use Azure SDK)". `VerifyImmutabilityConfigurationAsync` unconditionally sets `_immutabilityVerified = true` after `Task.Delay(10)`. |
| 59 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Storage/S3WormStorage.cs | 185 | sdk-audit | `_objectLockVerified` plain bool without volatile/Interlocked — stale reads across threads. |
| 60 | CRITICAL | TamperProof/Storage/S3WormStorage.cs | 187-288 | sdk-audit | Entire class is in-memory `Dictionary` simulation — comment: "Simulated storage for development/testing (in production, would use AWS SDK)". `VerifyObjectLockConfigurationAsync` unconditionally sets `_objectLockVerified = true`. Zero actual S3 Object Lock enforcement. |
| 61 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Storage/S3WormStorage.cs | 237 | sdk-audit | `EnforcementMode` returns `HardwareIntegrated` for Compliance mode — but entire provider is in-memory simulation. |
| 62 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Storage/S3WormStorage.cs | 319 | sdk-audit | `(int)data.Length` — silent overflow for streams >2GB. |
| 63 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/Storage/S3WormStorage.cs | 432-492 | sdk-audit | `async` methods with only synchronous `lock` blocks — `lock` inside async causes thread-pool starvation under load. |
| 64 | HIGH | TamperProof/TamperProofPlugin.cs | 150-161 | sdk-audit | Integrity violation alert `_ = Task.Run(...)` with `CancellationToken.None` — can outlive plugin disposal. No backpressure. |
| 65 | HIGH | TamperProof/TamperProofPlugin.cs | 349 | sdk-audit | `_ = Task.Run(() => ProcessBlockchainBatchAsync(ct))` — blockchain batch discarded. Anchor loss for all queued objects on exception. |
| 66 | LOW | TamperProof/TimeLock/CloudTimeLockProvider.cs | 284 | sdk-audit | `RequiredApprovals` not validated ≥2 — value 0 trivially bypasses multi-party gate. |
| 67 | HIGH | TamperProof/TimeLock/CloudTimeLockProvider.cs | 530-533,576-579 | sdk-audit | `DetectCloudProviderAsync` and `VerifyCloudRetentionExpiredAsync` swallow `OperationCanceledException` — cancelled verification returns `true`, incorrectly allowing lock release. |
| 68 | LOW | TamperProof/TimeLock/CloudTimeLockProvider.cs | 623-638 | sdk-audit | Content hash includes `UtcNow` timestamp — non-reproducible, useless for integrity verification. Same in Hsm/Software providers. |
| 69 | HIGH | TamperProof/TimeLock/HsmTimeLockProvider.cs | 464-494 | sdk-audit | AES-GCM wrapping key generated, used, then zeroed — no decryption path exists. Comment: "in production HSM, this key stays in hardware". Encrypted key material permanently irrecoverable. |
| 70 | LOW | TamperProof/TimeLock/RansomwareVaccinationService.cs | 469-553 | sdk-audit | All bus-coordination catch blocks return false/null with no exception logging — vaccination failures undiagnosable. |
| 71 | HIGH | TamperProof/TimeLock/SoftwareTimeLockProvider.cs | 203-217 | sdk-audit | MultiPartyApproval counts `approverIds.Length` with no cryptographic verification — any caller passing N arbitrary strings satisfies N-of-M approval. Same in Hsm/Cloud providers. |
| 72 | MEDIUM | TamperProof/TimeLock/TimeLockPolicyEngine.cs | 50-88 | sdk-audit | `OrderByDescending` on `ConcurrentBag` + `Regex.IsMatch` on every evaluation — full sort + regex compilation per call on hot path. |
| 73 | HIGH | TamperProof/TimeLock/TimeLockPolicyEngine.cs | 97-107 | sdk-audit | `ConcurrentBag<TimeLockRule>` duplicate-name check + `Add` not atomic — concurrent threads can insert duplicate rules. `RemoveRule` rebuilds without lock — concurrent adds dropped. |

| 43 | LOW | Transcoding.Media (29 files) | — | agent-scan | 29 files not fully audited — follow-up audit recommended |
| 44 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Execution/FfmpegExecutor.cs | 229-232 | sdk-audit | `process.Start()` return value not checked — `false` return causes downstream NullReferenceException. |
| 45 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Execution/FfmpegExecutor.cs | 359-385 | sdk-audit | `TryParseProgress` returns raw elapsed seconds, not 0-100 percentage — `progressCallback` documented as "percentage" receives values in thousands for long videos. |
| 46 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Execution/FfmpegTranscodeHelper.cs | - | sdk-audit | FFmpeg failure fallback to package taken silently with no log — callers can't distinguish real output from fallback. |
| 47 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Execution/MediaFormatDetector.cs | 63-65 | sdk-audit | EBML header <30 bytes falls through to `return true` (WebM) — MKV files misidentified as WebM. |
| 48 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Execution/TranscodePackageExecutor.cs | 107-131 | sdk-audit | `reader.ReadInt32()` for field lengths with no bounds check — malicious package causes 2GB allocation. |
| 49 | CRITICAL | Transcoding.Media/Execution/TranscodePackageExecutor.cs | 126-138 | sdk-audit | `ParsePackageAsync` sets `sourceData = Array.Empty<byte>()` — comment: "package format doesn't actually include the source data inline". FFmpeg receives empty stdin. All package transcoding broken. |
| 50 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/MediaTranscodingPlugin.cs | 178 | sdk-audit | Timer callback `ProcessQueue` may call async operations via sync-over-async — deadlock/starvation risk. |
| 51 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/MediaTranscodingPlugin.cs | 349-352,413-415 | sdk-audit | `DetectFormatByMagicBytesAsync` and `DetermineOfficeFormatAsync` swallow all exceptions including cancellation — "format unknown" indistinguishable from "file unreadable". |
| 52 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Camera/CameraFrameSource.cs | 125-133 | sdk-audit | `Dispose(bool)` explicitly does nothing — sync `using` blocks leak camera handle. Comment: "User must call DisposeAsync()". |
| 53 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/DdsTextureStrategy.cs | 321-328 | sdk-audit | `ParseDdsDimensions` no validation for negative/extreme width×height — integer overflow in mipmap calculations. |
| 54 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/DdsTextureStrategy.cs | 440-482 | sdk-audit | `GenerateMipmapChain` fills mipmaps with HMAC pseudo-random bytes, not actual BC1-BC7 block compression — GPU textures contain garbage pixel data. |
| 55 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/KtxTextureStrategy.cs | 99-100 | sdk-audit | `targetWidth`/`targetHeight` default 1024, no power-of-two validation — resolution 0 produces corrupt mipmap chain. |
| 56 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/KtxTextureStrategy.cs | 175-213 | sdk-audit | `GenerateThumbnailAsyncCore` writes SHA-256 hash as "thumb data" with proprietary `"KTXTHUMB"` header — not a valid image format. |
| 57 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/KtxTextureStrategy.cs | 260-266 | sdk-audit | `supercompressionScheme` maps both `"BasisLZ"` and `"UASTC"` to value `1` — UASTC should be `0` or `2` per KTX2 spec. Loaders misinterpret compression. |
| 58 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/GPUTexture/KtxTextureStrategy.cs | 269-275 | sdk-audit | `WriteKtx2Header` writes DFD/KVD/SGD offset and length fields all as zero — KTX2-conformant loaders fail to find sections. Structurally non-conformant files. |
| 59 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/AvifImageStrategy.cs | 108-137 | sdk-audit | Same missing input validation as WebP — no stream guard, no size limit for 65536x65536 format. |
| 60 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/AvifImageStrategy.cs | 279 | sdk-audit | `colorSpace = isHdr ? "nclx" : "nclx"` — both ternary branches identical. SDR path should use `"rICC"` or `"prof"`. |
| 61 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/AvifImageStrategy.cs | 430-462 | sdk-audit | `CompressWithAv1` fills buffer with HMAC pseudo-random bytes — not valid AV1 OBU bitstream. Same pattern in WebP `CompressWithVp8`/`CompressLossless`, all RAW `ApplyXxxProcessingPipeline`, and JPEG `ApplyDctCompression`. Every image/RAW transcode produces invalid output. |
| 62 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/JpegImageStrategy.cs | 91-95 | sdk-audit | Health check `isOperational = testPixel.Length == 4` — dead code, always true. Same in PngImageStrategy. |
| 63 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/JpegImageStrategy.cs | 313-321 | sdk-audit | Error message calls `DetermineQuality(options)` again instead of using existing `quality` local. |
| 64 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/JpegImageStrategy.cs | 591-623 | sdk-audit | HMAC compression simulation — `hmac.ComputeHash(BitConverter.GetBytes(blockIndex++))` tight loop allocates per-iteration. Same in Avif/WebP. |
| 65 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/WebPImageStrategy.cs | 111-118 | sdk-audit | `TranscodeAsyncCore` missing null/unreadable stream guard and 500MB size check present in JPEG/PNG siblings. |
| 66 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Image/WebPImageStrategy.cs | 123,209 | sdk-audit | `outputStream` not disposed on exception path before return — buffer held until GC. Same in JPEG/PNG. |
| 67 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/RAW/ArwRawStrategy.cs | 336-344 | sdk-audit | `DetectPixelShiftMode` guard `i + 8 < data.Length` but accesses `data[i+9]` — off-by-one out-of-bounds. |
| 68 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/RAW/Cr2RawStrategy.cs | 43-45 | sdk-audit | `CanonWhiteBalanceTag` and `CanonColorDataTag` both `0x4001` — dead constants. Actual parse at line 343-347 accesses `data[i+9]` beyond bounds check `data.Length - 8`. |
| 69 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/RAW/Cr2RawStrategy.cs | 373-378 | sdk-audit | `ExtractIsoSpeed` loop guard `data.Length - 6` but accesses `data[i+8]` and `data[i+9]` — out-of-bounds read for last 3 iterations. |
| 70 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/RAW/DngRawStrategy.cs | 275-285 | sdk-audit | `ValidateDngFormat` handles endianness but `ParseDngVersion`/`DetectLinearDng`/`ExtractCameraModel` assume little-endian — big-endian DNG returns garbage. |
| 71 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/RAW/DngRawStrategy.cs | 357-367 | sdk-audit | `ExtractColorMatrix` returns hardcoded sRGB D65 matrix, ignores `data` parameter — all DNG files process with same matrix regardless of camera. |
| 72 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Streaming/CmafStreamingStrategy.cs | 105-111 | sdk-audit | `targetResolution` smaller than lowest rung (640x360) — `applicableTracks` empty, fallback returns 640x360 track exceeding caller constraint. Same in DASH/HLS. |
| 73 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Streaming/CmafStreamingStrategy.cs | 296-306 | sdk-audit | `BuildFfmpegArguments` interpolates `options.VideoCodec`/`AudioCodec` without sanitization — FFmpeg argument injection if user-controlled. Same in DASH/HLS. |
| 74 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Streaming/DashStreamingStrategy.cs | 93-94 | sdk-audit | Health check `var isAccessible = Directory.Exists(segmentDir) \ |
| 75 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Streaming/DashStreamingStrategy.cs | 125-131 | sdk-audit | `ShutdownAsyncCore` comments claim cleanup but body is `return Task.CompletedTask`. Same in HLS. |
| 76 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/ThreeD/GltfModelStrategy.cs | 246-268 | sdk-audit | `ExtractGlbBinary` negative `jsonChunkLength` causes `binOffset < 20` — no guard. |
| 77 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/ThreeD/GltfModelStrategy.cs | 277-292 | sdk-audit | `ParseGltfJson` bare `catch { return new GltfRoot(); }` — malformed JSON silently produces empty root. Callers proceed with corrupt output. |
| 78 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/ThreeD/GltfModelStrategy.cs | 375-380 | sdk-audit | `GenerateBinaryBuffer` returns `SHA256.HashData(sourceBytes)` (32 bytes) — not actual GLB binary buffer. Accessor out-of-bounds in any glTF consumer. |
| 79 | LOW | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/ThreeD/UsdModelStrategy.cs | 230-290 | sdk-audit | `ParseUsdaScene` counts "variantSet"/"sublayer" keywords including in comments/strings — inflated counters. |
| 80 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/ThreeD/UsdModelStrategy.cs | 347-349 | sdk-audit | USDZ ZIP parsing — `compressedSize = 0` with data descriptor flag causes infinite loop. No data descriptor scan. |
| 81 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/AdvancedVideoStrategies.cs | 57-292 | sdk-audit | Public async methods contain no `await` — state machine allocated with no async work. CancellationToken never observed. |
| 82 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/AdvancedVideoStrategies.cs | 77-96,313-426 | sdk-audit | `Stereo3DVideoStrategy`, `Video360Strategy`, `VrVideoStrategy`, `HdrToneMappingStrategy` return empty `MemoryStream()` from all core methods. `StreamAsyncCore` returns `new Uri("about:blank")`. Complete stubs. |
| 83 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/AdvancedVideoStrategies.cs | 278-292 | sdk-audit | `RenderViewportFrameAsync` returns `RenderTimeMs = 0`, `MotionToPhotonMs = 0`, empty `FrameData`. Same: `ConvertToCubemapAsync` returns metadata but no cubemap data. |
| 84 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/AiProcessingStrategies.cs | 119-627 | sdk-audit | Five AI inference methods (`RunInferenceAsync`, `UpscaleAsync`, `DetectObjectsAsync`, `DetectFacesAsync`, `TranscribeAsync`) all return zeroed/empty results — `Confidence=0.0f`, `OutputData=Array.Empty<byte>()`, `Text=""`. |
| 85 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/AiProcessingStrategies.cs | 228-234 | sdk-audit | `AiUpscalingStrategy.Configure` no bounds validation — `tileOverlap >= tileSize`, `scaleFactor <= 0` cause downstream failures. |
| 86 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/Av1CodecStrategy.cs | 133 | sdk-audit | `new MemoryStream(1024*1024)` allocated then never used/disposed — `ExecuteOrPackageAsync` creates its own. Same in `H265CodecStrategy.cs:135`, `Vp9CodecStrategy.cs:123`, `VvcCodecStrategy.cs:119`. 1MB leak per transcode. |
| 87 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/Av1CodecStrategy.cs | 314-318 | sdk-audit | Two-pass FFmpeg args appended after single-pass args — missing pixel format, resolution, frame rate in second pass. Same in H264/H265 strategies. |
| 88 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/GpuAccelerationStrategies.cs | 27-35 | sdk-audit | `_activeEncoder`, `_selectedGpuIndex`, `_lastGpuScan` written under `_scanLock` but read without synchronization from `CheckGpuMemory`, `GetEncoderArgs`, `GetHealthStats`. Stale/mismatched encoder-GPU pairing on concurrent access. |
| 89 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/GpuAccelerationStrategies.cs | 329-333 | sdk-audit | `TryDetectNvidiaGpus` bare `catch { return false; }` — all exceptions silently suppressed including OOM. No logging for GPU detection failures. |
| 90 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/GpuAccelerationStrategies.cs | 336-343 | sdk-audit | `TryDetectIntelQsv` always returns `false` on Windows — naming lie. Intel QSV silently skipped on most common server platform. |
| 91 | HIGH | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/GpuAccelerationStrategies.cs | 355-364 | sdk-audit | `TranscodeAsyncCore` builds GPU encoder args then ignores them — returns empty `MemoryStream()`. `GenerateThumbnailAsyncCore` same. All GPU detection/selection logic is dead code. |
| 92 | CRITICAL | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/H264CodecStrategy.cs | 383-401 | sdk-audit | User-controlled `CustomMetadata` values (`watermarkText`, `watermarkImagePath`) interpolated into FFmpeg arguments without sanitization — command injection. Same in `H265CodecStrategy.cs:336-369` for `colorSpace`, `colorPrimaries`, `hdrTransfer`. |
| 93 | MEDIUM | Transcoding.Media/Strategies/Video/VvcCodecStrategy.cs | 214-224 | sdk-audit | `CheckEncoderAvailability` hardcodes `IsAvailable: true`, `Version: "1.11.0"` — no actual FFmpeg probe. Misleads package consumers. |

### UltimateAccessControl (409 findings: 25 critical, 139 high, 109 medium, 136 low)

| # | Sev | File | Line(s) | Source | Finding |

| 154 | HIGH | UltimateAccessControl/Features/AutomatedIncidentResponse.cs | 49-57 | sdk-audit | Failed containment actions only set string field — no logging, no metric. `PartialSuccess` status indistinguishable from total failure. `StopOnMatch` + failure prevents lower-priority playbooks from executing. |
| 155 | HIGH | UltimateAccessControl/Features/AutomatedIncidentResponse.cs | 113-170 | sdk-audit | Six containment actions (`BlockIpAsync`, `DisableAccountAsync`, `IsolateDeviceAsync`, `SendAlertAsync`, `CreateTicketAsync`, `QuarantineResourceAsync`) only set string fields — no actual firewall/ticketing/OS API calls. Security-critical no-ops. |
| 156 | MEDIUM | UltimateAccessControl/Features/BehavioralAnalysis.cs | 156-158 | sdk-audit | `profile.Locations.Count` can be 0 → divide-by-zero → `NaN`. `NaN < 0.1` is `false` — new-location deviation silently passes. |

| 158 | MEDIUM | UltimateAccessControl/Features/BehavioralAnalysis.cs | 232-265 | sdk-audit | `List<T>.RemoveAt(0)` as sliding window across 6 lists — O(n) per update. Same in `MlAnomalyDetection.UpdateProfile`. Use `Queue<T>`. |
| 159 | MEDIUM | UltimateAccessControl/Features/DlpEngine.cs | 64-91 | sdk-audit | No content size limit on DLP scan — 100MB input causes blocking regex across all policies. Also sync async pattern overhead. |
| 160 | HIGH | UltimateAccessControl/Features/DlpEngine.cs | 199-211 | sdk-audit | `ComputeSimpleHash` uses 32-bit polynomial hash for DLP content fingerprinting — predictable, trivially colliding. Use SHA-256. |

| 164 | HIGH | UltimateAccessControl/Features/PrivilegedAccessManager.cs | 57-65 | sdk-audit | Fire-and-forget `Task.Run` for JIT access revocation — silently fails on exception, cancelled by caller token, lost on process recycle. Security-critical revocation may never fire. |
