| 136 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Features/BackpressureHandling.cs | 120-147 | sdk-audit | Timer resource leak if constructor throws after creating first timer but before second. Already-created timer leaks. |
| 137 | CRITICAL | UltimateStreamingData/Features/BackpressureHandling.cs | 144 | sdk-audit | Timer callback uses async lambda — exceptions silently kill timer, permanently halting metrics reporting. |
| 138 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Features/BackpressureHandling.cs | 165-187 | sdk-audit | CreateChannel does not validate empty channelId — "" key can conflict with other unnamed channels. |
| 139 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Features/ComplexEventProcessing.cs | 217-246 | sdk-audit | ProcessBatchAsync awaits each event sequentially in loop. No concurrency benefit for large batches. |
| 140 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Features/ComplexEventProcessing.cs | 539-580 | sdk-audit | ConcurrentBag replaced non-atomically in PurgeExpired while AddEvent concurrently writes to old bag. Events silently lost. |
| 141 | CRITICAL | UltimateStreamingData/Features/StatefulStreamProcessing.cs | 157 | sdk-audit | Timer callback async lambda — exceptions silently kill checkpoint timer. No further checkpoints taken. |
| 142 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Features/StatefulStreamProcessing.cs | 414-441 | sdk-audit | RestoreFromCheckpointAsync silently skips entries with checksum mismatch — silent data loss after corruption. Should log/count. |
| 143 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Features/StatefulStreamProcessing.cs | 529-540 | sdk-audit | O(n) LRU eviction on every write via OrderBy().FirstOrDefault(). With MaxStateEntries=1M, severe latency spikes. Need min-heap/linked-list LRU. |
| 144 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Features/WatermarkManagement.cs | 227-267 | sdk-audit | PartitionWatermarkState fields mutated in ProcessEventAsync and timer callback without sync. DateTimeOffset (16-byte) can produce torn reads. |
| 145 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingBackpressureHandler.cs | 307 | sdk-audit | ApplyAdaptiveAsync returns DropOldest as "normal state no-op" — misleads callers into thinking DropOldest was applied. |
| 146 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 62-68 | sdk-audit | Non-volatile config fields (_partitionCount, _maxPartitions, etc.) read on multiple threads. EvaluateScaleDecisionAsync reads _partitionCount unsafely outside lock. |
| 147 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 288-298 | sdk-audit | PublishAsync atomicity gap — event never queued on exception but caller may retry causing potential duplicate if bus succeeded partially. |
| 148 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 321 | sdk-audit | "exactly-once" comment but AssignPartitions assigns all partitions to all consumers. eventSequence is local counter. No exactly-once. |
| 149 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 444-454 | sdk-audit | _partitionCount read without Volatile.Read/lock while ScalePartitions writes under _partitionLock. Stale read routes to uninitialized partition channel — null-ref or out-of-bounds. |
| 150 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 449 | sdk-audit | string.GetHashCode(Ordinal) for partition routing — non-deterministic across restarts. Same key routes to different partition after restart. Use SHA-256. |
| 151 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 526-534 | sdk-audit | AssignPartitions assigns ALL partitions to EVERY consumer regardless of group — no actual load-balanced partitioning. Rule 13 stub. |
| 152 | MEDIUM | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 558-580 | sdk-audit | Repeated GetAverageLatencyMs calls per partition on hot publish path. Minor but multiplied by partition count. |
| 153 | LOW | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 621 | sdk-audit | await Task.CompletedTask at end of method — unnecessary state machine allocation. |
| 154 | LOW | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 621 | sdk-audit | EvaluateScaleDecisionAsync async overhead for synchronous work. |
| 155 | HIGH | UltimateStreamingData/Scaling/StreamingScalingManager.cs | 627 | sdk-audit | Rule 13 — ScalePartitions adds channels but running consumers have fixed partition arrays. New partitions never consumed — scale-up has no real effect. |
